qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF via file://localhost bypass in GraphQL Asset mutation enables arbitrary local file read
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable component and root cause: this finding concerns GraphQL asset mutation URL scheme validation in Asset.php, while the advisory concerns SQL injection in the element-indexes/get-elements criteria[orderBy] ORDER BY handling.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described Craft CMS element index SQL injection in POST /index.php?p=admin/actions/element-indexes/get-elements via criteria[orderBy]. The only qualifying finding targets a separate GraphQL asset upload SSRF/local file read path.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-2453-mppf-46cj describes SQL injection through criteria[orderBy] on the element-indexes/get-elements endpoint, caused by unsanitized ORDER BY input.
ssrf-file-read-graphql-asset-upload/vulnerability_report.json describes GraphQL mutation save_<volumeHandle>_Asset with _file.url reaching Asset.php validateHostname() and Guzzle URL fetching.
The finding's own report tags the issue as GHSA-96pq-hxpw-rgh8 in the changelog context, not GHSA-2453-mppf-46cj.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding was present, so runtime tiering does not apply.
